topics.name: Candidate Business Ethics and Personal Wealth

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Good evening from Hofstra University in Hempstead, New York.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: I’m Lester Holt, anchor of “NBC Nightly News.”

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: I want to welcome you to the first presidential debate.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The participants tonight are Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: This debate is sponsored by the Commission on Presidential Debates, a nonpartisan, nonprofit organization.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The commission drafted tonight’s format, and the rules have been agreed to by the campaigns.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The 90-minute debate is divided into six segments, each 15 minutes long.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: We’ll explore three topic areas tonight: Achieving prosperity; America’s direction; and securing America.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: At the start of each segment, I will ask the same lead-off question to both candidates, and they will each have up to two minutes to respond.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: From that point until the end of the segment, we’ll have an open discussion.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The questions are mine and have not been shared with the commission or the campaigns.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The audience here in the room has agreed to remain silent so that we can focus on what the candidates are saying.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: I will invite you to applaud, however, at this moment, as we welcome the candidates: Democratic nominee for president of the United States, Hillary Clinton, and Republican nominee for president of the United States, Donald J. Trump.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: How are you, Donald?

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Good luck to you.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Well, I don’t expect us to cover all the issues of this campaign tonight, but I remind everyone, there are two more presidential debates scheduled.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: We are going to focus on many of the issues that voters tell us are most important, and we’re going to press for specifics.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: I am honored to have this role, but this evening belongs to the candidates and, just as important, to the American people.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Candidates, we look forward to hearing you articulate your policies and your positions, as well as your visions and your values.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: So, let’s begin.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: We’re calling this opening segment “Achieving Prosperity.”

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: And central to that is jobs.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: There are two economic realities in America today.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: There’s been a record six straight years of job growth, and new census numbers show incomes have increased at a record rate after years of stagnation.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: However, income inequality remains significant, and nearly half of Americans are living paycheck to paycheck.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Beginning with you, Secretary Clinton, why are you a better choice than your opponent to create the kinds of jobs that will put more money into the pockets of American works?

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, thank you, Lester, and thanks to Hofstra for hosting us.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: The central question in this election is really what kind of country we want to be and what kind of future we’ll build together.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Today is my granddaughter’s second birthday, so I think about this a lot.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: First, we have to build an economy that works for everyone, not just those at the top.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: That means we need new jobs, good jobs, with rising incomes.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I want us to invest in you. I want us to invest in your future.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: That means jobs in infrastructure, in advanced manufacturing, innovation and technology, clean, renewable energy, and small business, because most of the new jobs will come from small business.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: We also have to make the economy fairer.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: That starts with raising the national minimum wage and also guarantee, finally, equal pay for women’s work.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I also want to see more companies do profit-sharing.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: If you help create the profits, you should be able to share in them, not just the executives at the top.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: And I want us to do more to support people who are struggling to balance family and work.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I’ve heard from so many of you about the difficult choices you face and the stresses that you’re under.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: So let’s have paid family leave, earned sick days.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Let’s be sure we have affordable child care and debt-free college.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: How are we going to do it? We’re going to do it by having the wealthy pay their fair share and close the corporate loopholes.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Finally, we tonight are on the stage together, Donald Trump and I.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Donald, it’s good to be with you.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: You know, Donald was very fortunate in his life, and that’s all to his benefit.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: He started his business with $14 million, borrowed from his father, and he really believes that the more you help wealthy people, the better off we’ll be and that everything will work out from there.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I don’t buy that.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I have a different experience.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: My father was a small-businessman.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: He worked really hard.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Let me follow up with Mr. Trump, if you can.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: You’ve talked about creating 25 million jobs, and you’ve promised to bring back millions of jobs for Americans.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Well, for one thing — and before we start on that — my father gave me a very small loan in 1975, and I built it into a company that’s worth many, many billions of dollars...

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Our jobs are fleeing the country.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re going to Mexico.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re going to many other countries.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: You look at what China is doing to our country in terms of making our product.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re devaluing their currency, and there’s nobody in our government to fight them.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And we have a very good fight.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And we have a winning fight.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Because they’re using our country as a piggy bank to rebuild China, and many other countries are doing the same thing.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: So we’re losing our good jobs, so many of them.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: When you look at what’s happening in Mexico, a friend of mine who builds plants said it’s the eighth wonder of the world.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re building some of the biggest plants anywhere in the world, some of the most sophisticated, some of the best plants.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: With the United States, as he said, not so much.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: So Ford is leaving.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: You see that, their small car division leaving.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Thousands of jobs leaving Michigan, leaving Ohio.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re all leaving.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And we can’t allow it to happen anymore.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: As far as child care is concerned and so many other things, I think Hillary and I agree on that.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: We probably disagree a little bit as to numbers and amounts and what we’re going to do, but perhaps we’ll be talking about that later.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: But we have to stop our jobs from being stolen from us.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: We have to stop our companies from leaving the United States and, with it, firing all of their people.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: All you have to do is take a look at Carrier air conditioning in Indianapolis.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They left — fired 1,400 people.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They’re going to Mexico.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: So many hundreds and hundreds of companies are doing this.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: We cannot let it happen.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Under my plan, I’ll be reducing taxes tremendously, from 35 percent to 15 percent for companies, small and big businesses.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: That’s going to be a job creator like we haven’t seen since Ronald Reagan.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: It’s going to be a beautiful thing to watch.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Companies will come.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They will build.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: They will expand.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: New companies will start.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And I look very, very much forward to doing it.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: We have to renegotiate our trade deals, and we have to stop these countries from stealing our companies and our jobs.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Secretary Clinton, would you like to respond?

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, I think that trade is an important issue.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Of course, we are 5 percent of the world’s population; we have to trade with the other 95 percent.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: And we need to have smart, fair trade deals.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: We also, though, need to have a tax system that rewards work and not just financial transactions.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: And the kind of plan that Donald has put forth would be trickle-down economics all over again.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: In fact, it would be the most extreme version, the biggest tax cuts for the top percent of the people in this country than we’ve ever had.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I call it trumped-up trickle-down, because that’s exactly what it would be.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: That is not how we grow the economy.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: We just have a different view about what’s best for growing the economy, how we make investments that will actually produce jobs and rising incomes.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I think we come at it from somewhat different perspectives.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Mr. Trump, we’re talking about the burden that Americans have to pay, yet you have not released your tax returns.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: I don’t mind releasing — I’m under a routine audit.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And it’ll be released.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And — as soon as the audit’s finished, it will be released.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: I will release my tax returns — against my lawyer’s wishes — when she releases her 33,000 e-mails that have been deleted.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: I do.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: You know, I made a mistake using a private e- mail.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: And if I had to do it over again, I would, obviously, do it differently.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: But I’m not going to make any excuses.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: It was a mistake, and I take responsibility for that.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: That was more than a mistake.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: That was done purposely.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: We move into our next segment talking about America’s direction.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: And let’s start by talking about race.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: The share of Americans who say race relations are bad in this country is the highest it’s been in decades, much of it amplified by shootings of African-Americans by police, as we’ve seen recently in Charlotte and Tulsa.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, you’re right. Race remains a significant challenge in our country.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Unfortunately, race still determines too much, often determines where people live, determines what kind of education in their public schools they can get, and, yes, it determines how they’re treated in the criminal justice system.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Well, first of all, Secretary Clinton doesn’t want to use a couple of words, and that’s law and order.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: And we need law and order.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: If we don’t have it, we’re not going to have a country.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Mr. Trump, for five years, you perpetuated a false claim that the nation’s first black president was not a natural-born citizen.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: I’ll tell you very — well, just very simple to say.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Sidney Blumenthal works for the campaign and close — very close friend of Secretary Clinton.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: When I got involved, I didn’t fail.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: I got him to give the birth certificate.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: But it can’t be dismissed that easily.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: He has really started his political activity based on this racist lie that our first black president was not an American citizen.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Our next segment is called “Securing America.”

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, I think cyber security, cyber warfare will be one of the biggest challenges facing the next president...

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: As far as the cyber, I agree to parts of what Secretary Clinton said.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: I have a son. He’s 10 years old. He has computers. He is so good with these computers, it’s unbelievable.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Mr. Trump, this year Secretary Clinton became the first woman nominated for president by a major party.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: Earlier this month, you said she doesn’t have, quote, “a presidential look.”

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: She doesn’t have the look. She doesn’t have the stamina.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, as soon as he travels to 112 countries and negotiates a peace deal... he can talk to me about stamina.

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: But this is a man who has called women pigs, slobs and dogs...

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: And he called this woman “Miss Piggy.” Then he called her “Miss Housekeeping,” because she was Latina.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: Where did you find this?

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: One of you will not win this election. So my final question to you tonight, are you willing to accept the outcome as the will of the voters?

nodes.speaker: CLINTON
nodes.text: Well, I support our democracy. And sometimes you win, sometimes you lose. But I certainly will support the outcome of this election.

nodes.speaker: TRUMP
nodes.text: The answer is, if she wins, I will absolutely support her.

nodes.speaker: HOLT
nodes.text: All right. Well, that is going to do it for us. That concludes our debate for this evening, a spirit one.
